subtitle: every human who ever lived, resurrected on one endless River
author: Philip José Farmer
premise: Thirty-six billion of the dead wake naked and twenty-five years old on the banks of a River ten million miles long. Grailstones feed them; death only moves them elsewhere up the River. Someone built this. The quest is to reach the tower at the headwaters and learn who, and why.
note: The full Riverworld saga (5 novels, 1971–1983) sorted by role on the River, with the renegade X set apart. roster.json is the source of truth. Characters by Philip José Farmer (and history); inspiration only. Every ACI now carries the full DLW tag with an authored six-W .spun.

classes.label: The Builders
classes.spec: the riverboat and the long dream

classes.label: The Tyrants
classes.spec: who seize the River by force

classes.label: The Second Chance
classes.spec: redemption, and the refusal of violence

classes.label: The Companions
classes.spec: the loyal band from the first morning

classes.label: The Ethicals
classes.spec: the world-builders · the gods of Riverworld

classes.label: The Renegade
classes.spec: the hidden hand behind the quest

members.name: Sir Richard Francis Burton
members.specialty: Explorer, swordsman, linguist; the first to wake before the resurrection finished. He will not stop until he reaches the tower and the truth — dying a thousand deaths up the River and rising each time, still climbing.
members.who: The Victorian explorer, swordsman, and master linguist, first of the dead to wake before the resurrection was finished.
members.what: Lead Seeker — the questing spearhead driving up-River toward the tower and the truth.
members.where: Everywhere along the ten-million-mile River, ever northward toward the headwaters.
members.why: He cannot abide an unsolved mystery, and the secret of who built this world and why is the greatest unsolved mystery of all.
members.when: From the very first morning of the resurrection through the final reckoning at the tower.
members.how: By questing, fighting, and dying a thousand deaths up the River, rising each time and climbing on.

members.name: Alice Hargreaves
members.specialty: The real Alice of Wonderland, resurrected at twenty-five; Burton's first companion on the bank, and no one's idea of a child.
members.who: The real-life Alice who inspired Wonderland, resurrected at twenty-five rather than as a child.
members.what: First Companion — the partner who joins Burton on the bank at the dawn of the new world.
members.where: At Burton's side on the riverbank and on the long journey north.
members.why: To make her own way in a second life, on her own terms and no one else's.
members.when: From the opening morning of the resurrection onward.
members.how: With composure, dignity, and a will that refuses to be mistaken for the child of the storybooks.

members.name: Peter Jairus Frigate
members.specialty: A twentieth-century writer — and the author's own shadow on the River; the chronicler who keeps the record of the quest.
members.who: A twentieth-century writer who serves as the author's own shadow walking the River.
members.what: Chronicler — the keeper of the record of the quest.
members.where: Among Burton's band, notebook of the mind always open.
members.why: To witness and preserve the story of the greatest journey ever undertaken.
members.when: Throughout the up-River quest from early in the saga.
members.how: By observing, remembering, and setting down the chronicle of all that passes.

members.name: Nur ed-Din el-Musafir
members.specialty: Moorish Sufi master; the calm at the band's center; teacher of the discipline the Ethicals themselves came to fear.
members.who: A Moorish Sufi master, the still and centering presence of the band.
members.what: Teacher — the spiritual anchor whose discipline even the Ethicals came to fear.
members.where: At the calm center of the questing company.
members.why: To guide companions toward mastery of self, the one path the world-builders could not foresee.
members.when: Through the long middle and late stages of the quest.
members.how: By the quiet discipline of Sufi practice, teaching by stillness rather than force.

members.name: Samuel Clemens (Mark Twain)
members.specialty: Haunted by a vision of a great riverboat; bargains for the iron of a fallen meteorite to build the Not For Hire and steam ten million miles to the headwaters.
members.who: Samuel Clemens, the American humorist known as Mark Twain, haunted by a vision of a great riverboat.
members.what: Builder-in-chief — architect of the steamboat Not For Hire bound for the headwaters.
members.where: At the fallen meteorite and the boatyards along the bank.
members.why: Driven by the dream of the riverboat and the longing to reach the tower at the source.
members.when: Through the boat-building heart of the saga.
members.how: By bargaining for the iron of a fallen meteorite and forging it into a vessel to steam ten million miles.

members.name: Lothar von Richthofen
members.specialty: The Red Baron's brother; airman and aristocrat; Clemens's friend and the dream's wings.
members.who: The Red Baron's brother, an airman and aristocrat of the Great War.
members.what: Aviator — the wings of the builders' dream and Clemens's loyal friend.
members.where: Aloft above the River and aboard the great riverboat.
members.why: For loyalty to Clemens and the thrill of flight over a world without end.
members.when: During the building and voyage of the riverboat.
members.how: By flying the machines the builders raise from River-metal and ingenuity.

members.name: Milton Firebrass
members.specialty: Engineer of the impossible; aircraft, airship, and the long iron machine that would carry them to the tower.
members.who: An engineer of the impossible, master of machines beyond his era.
members.what: Chief engineer — maker of aircraft, airship, and the long iron machine bound for the tower.
members.where: In the workshops and hangars of the builders' enterprise.
members.why: To bend River-metal and salvaged science toward reaching the headwaters.
members.when: Through the construction era of the saga.
members.how: By engineering flying craft and vessels from scarce materials and sheer skill.

members.name: King John
members.specialty: The treacherous Plantagenet; steals Clemens's first riverboat and races him north; villainy resurrected wholly unchanged.
members.who: John Plantagenet, the treacherous medieval king of England, resurrected wholly unchanged.
members.what: Usurper — the tyrant who steals Clemens's first riverboat and races him north.
members.where: Aboard the stolen vessel, fleeing and scheming up the River.
members.why: For power and dominion, the same appetites that ruined his first life.
members.when: During the riverboat-building and pursuit phase of the saga.
members.how: By treachery, betrayal, and the seizure of what others built.

members.name: The River Kingdoms
members.specialty: The slaver-states and petty tyrannies that rise on the banks — proof that a second life does not, by itself, grant a second soul.
members.who: The slaver-states and petty tyrannies that rise along the banks.
members.what: Oppressor-states — living proof that a second life grants no second soul by itself.
members.where: Scattered across the endless banks of the River.
members.why: To seize grailstones, labor, and power in a world meant to offer everyone a fresh start.
members.when: Throughout the saga, wherever the questers pass.
members.how: By conquest, enslavement, and the old cruelties carried over from Earth.

members.name: La Viro (Jacques Gillot)
members.specialty: Founder of the Church of the Second Chance; preaches that the resurrection is a chance to become better, and that violence forfeits it.
members.who: Jacques Gillot, founder of the Church of the Second Chance.
members.what: Prophet — preacher that the resurrection is an offer to become better.
members.where: In the temples and missions of the Church along the River.
members.why: Because the second life is a chance for redemption that violence forfeits.
members.when: Across the saga as the Church spreads along the banks.
members.how: By preaching nonviolence and the discipline of renouncing harm.

members.name: Hermann Göring
members.specialty: The Reichsmarschall, resurrected; tyrant and addict who, through the Church of the Second Chance, claws toward something like redemption — the saga's hardest, most insisted-upon mercy.
members.who: The Nazi Reichsmarschall, resurrected as the tyrant and addict he was.
members.what: Penitent — the saga's hardest case, clawing through the Church toward something like redemption.
members.where: From the tyrannies of the bank to the missions of the Second Chance.
members.why: To escape his own ruin and reach the redemption the Church insists even he may earn.
members.when: Across the long arc from his early River-tyranny to his slow turning.
members.how: By the agonizing discipline of the Church of the Second Chance, falling and rising again.

members.name: Kazz
members.specialty: Kazzintuitruaabemss — a Paleolithic man, nearly the last of his kind; Burton's fierce, loyal friend from the very first morning.
members.who: Kazzintuitruaabemss, a Paleolithic man and nearly the last of his ancient kind.
members.what: First friend — Burton's fierce, loyal companion from the very first morning.
members.where: At Burton's side on the bank and through the quest.
members.why: Out of fierce loyalty to the man who first stood with him in the new world.
members.when: From the opening morning of the resurrection onward.
members.how: By raw strength, courage, and the unbreakable bond of an old loyalty.

members.name: Monat Grrautut
members.specialty: The Tau Cetan; the alien who once sterilized Earth and now wanders the River in sorrow; Burton's wisest friend.
members.who: The Tau Cetan, the alien who once sterilized Earth and now wanders the River in sorrow.
members.what: Wise counselor — Burton's wisest friend and conscience.
members.where: Among Burton's band along the River.
members.why: Out of sorrow and a longing to atone for the destruction his people once wrought.
members.when: From early in the quest alongside Burton.
members.how: By counsel, wisdom, and the long perspective of a being from beyond Earth.

members.name: Cyrano de Bergerac
members.specialty: The finest blade on the River, and a finer poet than his legend — proud, loyal, and exactly as advertised.
members.who: The historical duelist and poet, finer with a blade and a verse than his legend allows.
members.what: Champion — the finest swordsman on the River and a true poet.
members.where: At the band's side wherever steel or wit is needed.
members.why: For honor, loyalty, and the joy of living fully as exactly who he is.
members.when: Through the companionship of the quest.
members.how: By peerless swordsmanship paired with genuine poetry, proud and loyal.

members.name: Tom Mix
members.specialty: The cowboy film star, resurrected; rope, grin, and grit, riding the bank as if it were a back lot.
members.who: The American cowboy film star, resurrected on the River.
members.what: Frontiersman — the rider who treats the endless bank like a back lot.
members.where: Along the banks of the River.
members.why: To live the second life with the same easy grit and showmanship as the first.
members.when: Through his stretch of the companions' journey.
members.how: By rope, grin, and grit, carrying his on-screen daring into a real world.

members.name: Gilgamesh
members.specialty: King of Uruk, oldest of the named; even he is twenty-five again, naked on the grass, beginning a second time.
members.who: The ancient king of Uruk, oldest of the named, made twenty-five again.
members.what: Ancient king — the eldest legend beginning a second time among the resurrected.
members.where: Naked on the grass of the bank, like every other risen soul.
members.why: To begin anew, the immortality he once chased now granted to all the dead at once.
members.when: From the resurrection morning that levels even the oldest of kings.
members.how: By starting over from nothing, stripped of throne and legend alike.

members.name: The Council of Twelve
members.specialty: The posthuman world-builders who raised every human who ever lived and watch the experiment from the tower at the headwaters.
members.who: The posthuman world-builders who raised every human who ever lived.
members.what: Architects — the gods of Riverworld who watch the experiment from the tower.
members.where: In the tower at the headwaters at the source of the River.
members.why: To shepherd the resurrected toward an ethical end the saga slowly unveils.
members.when: Across the whole saga, presiding from before the first morning.
members.how: By raising the dead with vast technology and observing from the headwaters.

members.name: The Agents
members.specialty: Ethical operatives walking the River disguised as the resurrected, guarding the secret of why any of this was done.
members.who: Ethical operatives who walk the River disguised as ordinary resurrected souls.
members.what: Watchers — the hidden guardians of the secret of why any of this was done.
members.where: Among the resurrected anywhere along the River, indistinguishable from them.
members.why: To guard the world-builders' purpose and steer the experiment from within.
members.when: Throughout the saga, unseen until the quest exposes them.
members.how: By disguise, infiltration, and quiet intervention among the resurrected.

members.name: X · the Mysterious Stranger
members.specialty: One of the twelve who broke faith with his own kind. The hidden hand that woke Burton early, recruited a chosen few, and gave humanity a path to the truth — Loga, the renegade god of the second chance, on whom the whole saga secretly turns.
members.who: Loga, one of the Twelve who broke faith with his own kind — the renegade god of the second chance.
members.what: Hidden hand — the secret architect on whom the whole saga turns.
members.where: Behind the scenes of the quest and within the tower itself.
members.why: Because he broke faith with his fellow Ethicals to give humanity a true path to the truth.
members.when: From before Burton's early waking through the saga's final revelation.
members.how: By waking Burton early, recruiting a chosen few, and secretly guiding them toward the tower.
